verses.text_hebrew: וַיְהִ֡י בְּיוֹם֩ כַּלּ֨וֹת מֹשֶׁ֜ה לְהָקִ֣ים אֶת־הַמִּשְׁכָּ֗ן וַיִּמְשַׁ֨ח אֹת֜וֹ וַיְקַדֵּ֤שׁ אֹתוֹ֙ וְאֶת־כׇּל־כֵּלָ֔יו וְאֶת־הַמִּזְבֵּ֖חַ וְאֶת־כׇּל־כֵּלָ֑יו וַיִּמְשָׁחֵ֖ם וַיְקַדֵּ֥שׁ אֹתָֽם׃
verses.text_kjv: And it came to pass on the day that Moses had fully set up the tabernacle, and had anointed it, and sanctified it, and all the instruments thereof, both the altar and all the vessels thereof, and had anointed them, and sanctified them;
verses.rendering: On the day Moses finished setting up the Tabernacle, he anointed and consecrated it along with all its equipment — the altar and all its utensils — anointing and consecrating everything.
translator_notes: The word kallot (completing, finishing) connects the Tabernacle's construction to its commissioning. The verbs vayimshach (he anointed) and vayeqaddesh (he consecrated) appear twice — first for the mishkan (Tabernacle) and its kelim (vessels/equipment), then for the mizbe'ach (altar) and its vessels — emphasizing the thorough consecration of every component.

key_terms.semantic_range: dwelling place, tent-sanctuary, portable temple, God's earthly residence
key_terms.note: From the root sh-k-n (to dwell, settle). The mishkan is God's dwelling among the people — not merely a worship tent but the locus of divine presence on earth.

verses.text_hebrew: וַיַּקְרִ֙יבוּ֙ נְשִׂיאֵ֣י יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל רָאשֵׁ֖י בֵּ֣ית אֲבֹתָ֑ם הֵ֚ם נְשִׂיאֵ֣י הַמַּטֹּ֔ת הֵ֥ם הָעֹמְדִ֖ים עַל־הַפְּקֻדִֽים׃
verses.text_kjv: That the princes of Israel, heads of the house of their fathers, who were the princes of the tribes, and were over them that were numbered, offered:
verses.rendering: The leaders of Israel — the heads of their ancestral households, the tribal leaders who had overseen the census — came forward with offerings.
translator_notes: The nesi'ei Yisra'el (leaders of Israel) are identified three ways: ra'shei beit avotam (heads of ancestral houses), nesi'ei hammattot (tribal leaders), and ha'omedim al-hapequdim (those overseeing the census). These are the same twelve leaders listed in Numbers 1:5-16 who supervised the population count.

key_terms.semantic_range: princes, chiefs, rulers, elevated ones, tribal heads
key_terms.note: From the root n-s-' (to lift, carry, elevate). A nasi is one who is 'raised up' — elevated to leadership. The term avoids the royal connotations of melekh (king), emphasizing representative rather than monarchic authority.

verses.text_hebrew: וַיָּבִ֨יאוּ אֶת־קׇרְבָּנָ֜ם לִפְנֵ֣י יְהֹוָ֗ה שֵׁשׁ־עֶגְלֹ֥ת צָב֙ וּשְׁנֵ֣י עָשָׂ֣ר בָּקָ֔ר עֲגָלָ֛ה עַל־שְׁנֵ֥י הַנְּשִׂאִ֖ים וְשׁ֣וֹר לְאֶחָ֑ד וַיַּקְרִ֥יבוּ אוֹתָ֖ם לִפְנֵ֥י הַמִּשְׁכָּֽן׃
verses.text_kjv: And they brought their offering before the LORD, six covered wagons, and twelve oxen; a wagon for two of the princes, and for each one an ox: and they brought them before the tabernacle.
verses.rendering: They brought their offering before the LORD: six covered wagons and twelve oxen — one wagon for every two leaders and one ox from each — and presented them in front of the Tabernacle.
translator_notes: The eglot tsav (covered wagons — possibly canopied or enclosed carts) are practical gifts for Tabernacle transport. The ratio — agalah al-shenei hannesi'im veshor le'echad (one wagon per two leaders, one ox per leader) — demonstrates collaborative generosity. Twelve leaders produce six wagons, each pair sharing a contribution.

verses.text_hebrew: וַיֹּ֥אמֶר יְהֹוָ֖ה אֶל־מֹשֶׁ֥ה לֵּאמֹֽר׃
verses.text_kjv: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,
verses.rendering: The LORD spoke to Moses:
translator_notes: The standard revelatory formula vayyomer YHWH el-Mosheh (the LORD said to Moses) introduces divine authorization for accepting the leaders' spontaneous gifts — confirming that their initiative aligns with God's will.

verses.text_hebrew: קַ֚ח מֵֽאִתָּ֔ם וְהָי֕וּ לַעֲבֹ֕ד אֶת־עֲבֹדַ֖ת אֹ֣הֶל מוֹעֵ֑ד וְנָתַתָּ֤ה אוֹתָם֙ אֶל־הַלְוִיִּ֔ם אִ֖ישׁ כְּפִ֥י עֲבֹדָתֽוֹ׃
verses.text_kjv: Take it of them, that they may be to do the service of the tabernacle of the congregation; and thou shalt give them unto the Levites, to every man according to his service.
verses.rendering: "Accept these from them — they will be used in the service of the Tent of Meeting. Give them to the Levites, to each division according to its duties."
translator_notes: God instructs Moses to distribute the wagons for the avodah (service, work) of the ohel mo'ed (Tent of Meeting). The principle ish kefi avodato (each man according to his service) means resources are allocated based on practical need rather than rank or prestige.

verses.text_hebrew: וַיִּקַּ֣ח מֹשֶׁ֔ה אֶת־הָעֲגָלֹ֖ת וְאֶת־הַבָּקָ֑ר וַיִּתֵּ֥ן אוֹתָ֖ם אֶל־הַלְוִיִּֽם׃
verses.text_kjv: And Moses took the wagons and the oxen, and gave them unto the Levites.
verses.rendering: So Moses accepted the wagons and oxen and distributed them to the Levites.
translator_notes: Moses executes the divine instruction precisely: vayyiqqach (he took/accepted) and vayyitten (he gave/distributed). The immediate obedience pattern — command followed by exact execution — is characteristic of Tabernacle narratives throughout Exodus and Numbers.

verses.text_hebrew: אֵ֣ת ׀ שְׁתֵּ֣י הָעֲגָל֗וֹת וְאֵת֙ אַרְבַּ֣עַת הַבָּקָ֔ר נָתַ֖ן לִבְנֵ֣י גֵרְשׁ֑וֹן כְּפִ֖י עֲבֹדָתָֽם׃
verses.text_kjv: Two wagons and four oxen he gave unto the sons of Gershon, according to their service:
verses.rendering: He gave two wagons and four oxen to the Gershonites, suited to their duties.
translator_notes: Gershon's clan receives the smaller allotment — two wagons and four oxen — because their responsibilities involved the lighter Tabernacle coverings: the curtains, hangings, and fabric screens (Num 4:25-26). Fewer wagons were needed for textiles than for the heavy structural components assigned to Merari.

verses.text_hebrew: וְאֵ֣ת ׀ אַרְבַּ֣ע הָעֲגָלֹ֗ת וְאֵת֙ שְׁמֹנַ֣ת הַבָּקָ֔ר נָתַ֖ן לִבְנֵ֣י מְרָרִ֑י כְּפִי֙ עֲבֹ֣דָתָ֔ם בְּיַד֙ אִֽיתָמָ֔ר בֶּֽן־אַהֲרֹ֖ן הַכֹּהֵֽן׃
verses.text_kjv: And four wagons and eight oxen he gave unto the sons of Merari, according unto their service, under the hand of Ithamar the son of Aaron the priest.
verses.rendering: He gave four wagons and eight oxen to the Merarites, suited to their duties under the supervision of Ithamar son of Aaron the priest.
translator_notes: Merari receives double Gershon's allotment — four wagons and eight oxen — because their duties involved the heavy structural framework: boards, crossbars, pillars, and bases (Num 4:31-32). Their work is supervised beyad Itamar (under the hand/authority of Ithamar), Aaron's youngest surviving son after Nadab and Abihu's death.

verses.text_hebrew: וְלִבְנֵ֥י קְהָ֖ת לֹ֣א נָתָ֑ן כִּֽי־עֲבֹדַ֤ת הַקֹּ֙דֶשׁ֙ עֲלֵהֶ֔ם בַּכָּתֵ֖ף יִשָּֽׂאוּ׃
verses.text_kjv: But unto the sons of Kohath he gave none: because the service of the sanctuary belonging unto them was that they should bear upon their shoulders.
verses.rendering: But to the Kohathites he gave nothing, because the sacred service assigned to them required carrying the holy objects on their shoulders.
translator_notes: The Kohathites receive no wagons because the avodat haqqodesh (service of the sacred objects) — the Ark, the table, the lampstand, the altars — must be carried bakkattef (on the shoulder), by direct human contact. No cart or animal may transport the most sacred items; they demand the dignity of personal bearing. This principle was violated with fatal consequences when David used a cart in 2 Samuel 6:3-7.

verses.text_hebrew: וַיַּקְרִ֣יבוּ הַנְּשִׂאִ֗ים אֵ֚ת חֲנֻכַּ֣ת הַמִּזְבֵּ֔חַ בְּי֖וֹם הִמָּשַׁ֣ח אֹת֑וֹ וַיַּקְרִ֧יבוּ הַנְּשִׂיאִ֛ם אֶת־קׇרְבָּנָ֖ם לִפְנֵ֥י הַמִּזְבֵּֽחַ׃
verses.text_kjv: And the princes offered for dedicating of the altar in the day that it was anointed, even the princes offered their offering before the altar.
verses.rendering: The leaders also brought their dedication offerings for the altar on the day it was anointed. The leaders presented their offerings before the altar.
translator_notes: The word chanukkat (dedication, inauguration) — from the root ch-n-k meaning 'to dedicate, initiate' — is the same word that gives us the festival of Hanukkah. Beyond the wagons and oxen, the leaders now bring qorbanam (their offerings) specifically for consecrating the mizbe'ach (altar). The repetition of vayyaqrivu hannesi'im (the leaders offered) emphasizes their initiative.

key_terms.note: From ch-n-k (to dedicate, inaugurate). This word later names the festival of Hanukkah (164 BCE), which celebrated the rededication of the Second Temple altar — an intentional echo of this Numbers 7 dedication.

verses.text_hebrew: וַיֹּ֥אמֶר יְהֹוָ֖ה אֶל־מֹשֶׁ֑ה נָשִׂ֨יא אֶחָ֜ד לַיּ֗וֹם נָשִׂ֤יא אֶחָד֙ לַיּ֔וֹם יַקְרִ֙יבוּ֙ אֶת־קׇרְבָּנָ֔ם לַחֲנֻכַּ֖ת הַמִּזְבֵּֽחַ׃ {ס}
verses.text_kjv: And the LORD said unto Moses, They shall offer their offering, each prince on his day, for the dedicating of the altar.
verses.rendering: The LORD said to Moses, "They are to present their offerings one leader per day, one leader per day, for the dedication of the altar."
translator_notes: The phrase nasi echad layyom nasi echad layyom (one leader per day, one leader per day) is deliberately repetitive — mirroring the twelve-day repetitive structure that follows. Each tribe receives its own dedicated day, preventing any leader from being overshadowed and giving each offering full ceremonial attention. The twelve-day sequence begins in verse 12.

verses.text_hebrew: וַיְהִ֗י הַמַּקְרִ֛יב בַּיּ֥וֹם הָרִאשׁ֖וֹן אֶת־קׇרְבָּנ֑וֹ נַחְשׁ֥וֹן בֶּן־עַמִּינָדָ֖ב לְמַטֵּ֥ה יְהוּדָֽה׃
verses.text_kjv: And he that offered his offering the first day was Nahshon the son of Amminadab, of the tribe of Judah:
verses.rendering: The one who presented his offering on the first day was Nahshon son of Amminadab, from the tribe of Judah.
translator_notes: Day 1: Nahshon (nachshon, possibly from nachash 'serpent' or 'diviner') son of Amminadab ('my kinsman is generous'), Judah's leader. Judah presents first, reflecting its preeminent position at the head of the camp formation (Num 2:3) and the march order (Num 10:14). Nahshon is later listed in the genealogy of King David (Ruth 4:20) and Jesus (Matt 1:4).

verses.text_hebrew: וְקׇרְבָּנ֞וֹ קַֽעֲרַת־כֶּ֣סֶף אַחַ֗ת שְׁלֹשִׁ֣ים וּמֵאָה֮ מִשְׁקָלָהּ֒ מִזְרָ֤ק אֶחָד֙ כֶּ֔סֶף שִׁבְעִ֥ים שֶׁ֖קֶל בְּשֶׁ֣קֶל הַקֹּ֑דֶשׁ שְׁנֵיהֶ֣ם ׀ מְלֵאִ֗ים סֹ֛לֶת בְּלוּלָ֥ה בַשֶּׁ֖מֶן לְמִנְחָֽה׃
verses.text_kjv: And his offering was one silver charger, the weight thereof was an hundred and thirty shekels, one silver bowl of seventy shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary; both of them were full of fine flour mingled with oil for a meat offering:
verses.rendering: His offering was one silver plate weighing one hundred thirty shekels, one silver basin of seventy shekels by the sanctuary standard — both filled with fine flour mixed with oil as a grain offering;
translator_notes: Nahshon's silver vessels for Judah (Day 1): the qa'arat (plate/platter, 130 shekels) and mizraq (sprinkling basin, 70 shekels) contain solet belulah vashemen (fine flour mixed with oil) — the minchah or grain offering. The combined silver weight of 200 shekels per tribe will total 2,400 shekels across all twelve.

verses.text_hebrew: כַּ֥ף אַחַ֛ת עֲשָׂרָ֥ה זָהָ֖ב מְלֵאָ֥ה קְטֹֽרֶת׃
verses.text_kjv: One spoon of ten shekels of gold, full of incense:
verses.rendering: one gold ladle of ten shekels, filled with incense;
translator_notes: Judah's gold kaf (ladle — literally 'palm,' a palm-shaped vessel, 10 shekels of gold) holds qetoret (incense) — fragrant spices representing worship ascending before the LORD. The gold material distinguishes this vessel from the silver plates.

verses.text_hebrew: פַּ֣ר אֶחָ֞ד בֶּן־בָּקָ֗ר אַ֧יִל אֶחָ֛ד כֶּֽבֶשׂ־אֶחָ֥ד בֶּן־שְׁנָת֖וֹ לְעֹלָֽה׃
verses.text_kjv: One young bullock, one ram, one lamb of the first year, for a burnt offering:
verses.rendering: one young bull, one ram, one male lamb in its first year, as a burnt offering;
translator_notes: Judah's olah (burnt offering, ascending offering): par ben-baqar (young bull), ayil (ram), keves ben-shenato (yearling male lamb) — three animals consumed entirely on the altar, representing complete dedication to God.

verses.text_hebrew: שְׂעִיר־עִזִּ֥ים אֶחָ֖ד לְחַטָּֽאת׃
verses.text_kjv: One kid of the goats for a sin offering:
verses.rendering: one male goat as a purification offering;
translator_notes: Judah's chattat (purification offering): one se'ir izzim (male goat) — addressing any ritual contamination in the tribe's relationship to the newly dedicated sanctuary.

verses.text_hebrew: וּלְזֶ֣בַח הַשְּׁלָמִים֮ בָּקָ֣ר שְׁנַ֒יִם֒ אֵילִ֤ם חֲמִשָּׁה֙ עַתּוּדִ֣ים חֲמִשָּׁ֔ה כְּבָשִׂ֥ים בְּנֵֽי־שָׁנָ֖ה חֲמִשָּׁ֑ה זֶ֛ה קׇרְבַּ֥ן נַחְשׁ֖וֹן בֶּן־עַמִּינָדָֽב׃ {פ}
verses.text_kjv: And for a sacrifice of peace offerings, two oxen, five rams, five he goats, five lambs of the first year: this was the offering of Nahshon the son of Amminadab.
verses.rendering: and for a fellowship sacrifice: two cattle, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs in their first year. This was the offering of Nahshon son of Amminadab.
translator_notes: Nahshon's zevach hashelamim (fellowship sacrifice) concludes Judah's Day 1 offering with the largest animal component: two cattle, five rams, five male goats, five yearling lambs — seventeen animals for communal feasting. The shelamim is the only offering category where the worshiper eats a portion, making it a shared meal between God, priest, and people.

verses.text_hebrew: בַּיּוֹם֙ הַשֵּׁנִ֔י הִקְרִ֖יב נְתַנְאֵ֣ל בֶּן־צוּעָ֑ר נְשִׂ֖יא יִשָּׂשכָֽר׃
verses.text_kjv: On the second day Nethaneel the son of Zuar, prince of Issachar, did offer:
verses.rendering: On the second day, Nethanel son of Zuar, the leader of Issachar, brought his offering.
translator_notes: Day 2: Nethanel (netan'el, 'God has given') son of Zuar (tsu'ar, 'small/insignificant'), Issachar's leader. Issachar follows Judah as part of the eastern camp division (Num 2:5). The name 'God has given' is fitting for a chapter about voluntary giving.

verses.text_hebrew: הִקְרִ֨ב אֶת־קׇרְבָּנ֜וֹ קַֽעֲרַת־כֶּ֣סֶף אַחַ֗ת שְׁלֹשִׁ֣ים וּמֵאָה֮ מִשְׁקָלָהּ֒ מִזְרָ֤ק אֶחָד֙ כֶּ֔סֶף שִׁבְעִ֥ים שֶׁ֖קֶל בְּשֶׁ֣קֶל הַקֹּ֑דֶשׁ שְׁנֵיהֶ֣ם ׀ מְלֵאִ֗ים סֹ֛לֶת בְּלוּלָ֥ה בַשֶּׁ֖מֶן לְמִנְחָֽה׃
verses.text_kjv: He offered for his offering one silver charger, the weight whereof was an hundred and thirty shekels, one silver bowl of seventy shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary; both of them full of fine flour mingled with oil for a meat offering:
verses.rendering: He presented as his offering one silver plate weighing one hundred thirty shekels, one silver basin of seventy shekels according to the sanctuary standard — both filled with fine flour blended with oil for a grain offering;
translator_notes: Nethanel's silver vessels for Issachar (Day 2): identical weights of 130 and 70 shekels for the qa'arat and mizraq, filled with the minchah (grain offering) mixture. The identical quantities establish a pattern: every tribe gives the same, demonstrating equality before God.

verses.text_hebrew: כַּ֥ף אַחַ֛ת עֲשָׂרָ֥ה זָהָ֖ב מְלֵאָ֥ה קְטֹֽרֶת׃
verses.text_kjv: One spoon of gold of ten shekels, full of incense:
verses.rendering: a single gold ladle weighing ten shekels, full of incense;
translator_notes: Issachar's gold kaf of qetoret (incense) — the same ten-shekel standard as Judah's, confirming equal dignity among the tribes in their approach to God.

verses.text_hebrew: פַּ֣ר אֶחָ֞ד בֶּן־בָּקָ֗ר אַ֧יִל אֶחָ֛ד כֶּֽבֶשׂ־אֶחָ֥ד בֶּן־שְׁנָת֖וֹ לְעֹלָֽה׃
verses.text_kjv: One young bullock, one ram, one lamb of the first year, for a burnt offering:
verses.rendering: one young bull, one ram, and one yearling male lamb for a burnt offering;
translator_notes: Issachar's olah: the identical three-animal set (bull, ram, yearling lamb) as Judah's — no tribe's ascending offering differs from another's in this dedication sequence.

verses.text_hebrew: שְׂעִיר־עִזִּ֥ים אֶחָ֖ד לְחַטָּֽאת׃
verses.text_kjv: One kid of the goats for a sin offering:
verses.rendering: one male goat for a purification offering;
translator_notes: Issachar's chattat: one male goat — each tribe requires its own purification offering regardless of its size or status in the camp.

verses.text_hebrew: וּלְזֶ֣בַח הַשְּׁלָמִים֮ בָּקָ֣ר שְׁנַ֒יִם֒ אֵילִ֤ם חֲמִשָּׁה֙ עַתֻּדִ֣ים חֲמִשָּׁ֔ה כְּבָשִׂ֥ים בְּנֵי־שָׁנָ֖ה חֲמִשָּׁ֑ה זֶ֛ה קׇרְבַּ֥ן נְתַנְאֵ֖ל בֶּן־צוּעָֽר׃ {פ}
verses.text_kjv: And for a sacrifice of peace offerings, two oxen, five rams, five he goats, five lambs of the first year: this was the offering of Nethaneel the son of Zuar.
verses.rendering: For a fellowship sacrifice: two cattle, five rams, five male goats, five yearling male lambs. This was the offering of Nethanel son of Zuar.
translator_notes: Nethanel's shelamim closes Issachar's Day 2 offering — identical quantities to Judah's, reinforcing the theme of tribal equality before the LORD in the dedication ceremony.

verses.text_hebrew: בַּיּוֹם֙ הַשְּׁלִישִׁ֔י נָשִׂ֖יא לִבְנֵ֣י זְבוּלֻ֑ן אֱלִיאָ֖ב בֶּן־חֵלֹֽן׃
verses.text_kjv: On the third day Eliab the son of Helon, prince of the children of Zebulun, did offer:
verses.rendering: On the third day, Eliab son of Helon, leader of the Zebulunites, presented his offering.
translator_notes: Day 3: Eliab (eli'av, 'my God is father') son of Helon (chelon, possibly 'strong' or 'valiant'), Zebulun's leader. Zebulun completes the eastern camp triad alongside Judah and Issachar (Num 2:7).

verses.text_hebrew: קׇרְבָּנ֞וֹ קַֽעֲרַת־כֶּ֣סֶף אַחַ֗ת שְׁלֹשִׁ֣ים וּמֵאָה֮ מִשְׁקָלָהּ֒ מִזְרָ֤ק אֶחָד֙ כֶּ֔סֶף שִׁבְעִ֥ים שֶׁ֖קֶל בְּשֶׁ֣קֶל הַקֹּ֑דֶשׁ שְׁנֵיהֶ֣ם ׀ מְלֵאִ֗ים סֹ֛לֶת בְּלוּלָ֥ה בַשֶּׁ֖מֶן לְמִנְחָֽה׃
verses.text_kjv: His offering was one silver charger, the weight whereof was an hundred and thirty shekels, one silver bowl of seventy shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary; both of them full of fine flour mingled with oil for a meat offering:
verses.rendering: His offering: a silver plate of one hundred thirty shekels weight, a silver basin of seventy shekels by the sanctuary standard, each filled with fine flour stirred with oil as a grain offering;
translator_notes: Eliab's silver vessels for Zebulun (Day 3): the identical qa'arat and mizraq weights with solet — the repetition itself is the text's theological statement about tribal parity. No tribe receives or gives more than any other.

verses.text_hebrew: כַּ֥ף אַחַ֛ת עֲשָׂרָ֥ה זָהָ֖ב מְלֵאָ֥ה קְטֹֽרֶת׃
verses.text_kjv: One golden spoon of ten shekels, full of incense:
verses.rendering: one gold ladle, ten shekels in weight, filled with incense;
translator_notes: Zebulun's gold kaf of qetoret — the third of twelve identical incense offerings, each tribe's worship rising equally before the LORD.

verses.text_hebrew: פַּ֣ר אֶחָ֞ד בֶּן־בָּקָ֗ר אַ֧יִל אֶחָ֛ד כֶּֽבֶשׂ־אֶחָ֥ד בֶּן־שְׁנָת֖וֹ לְעֹלָֽה׃
verses.text_kjv: One young bullock, one ram, one lamb of the first year, for a burnt offering:
verses.rendering: a young bull, a ram, and a first-year male lamb as a burnt offering;
translator_notes: Zebulun's olah: bull, ram, yearling lamb — the eastern camp's final burnt offering on Day 3, completing the first triad of tribes.

verses.text_hebrew: שְׂעִיר־עִזִּ֥ים אֶחָ֖ד לְחַטָּֽאת׃
verses.text_kjv: One kid of the goats for a sin offering:
verses.rendering: one male goat as a purification offering;
translator_notes: Zebulun's chattat: one male goat as purification — the third tribe's contribution to the twelve-day dedication sequence.

verses.text_hebrew: וּלְזֶ֣בַח הַשְּׁלָמִים֮ בָּקָ֣ר שְׁנַ֒יִם֒ אֵילִ֤ם חֲמִשָּׁה֙ עַתֻּדִ֣ים חֲמִשָּׁ֔ה כְּבָשִׂ֥ים בְּנֵי־שָׁנָ֖ה חֲמִשָּׁ֑ה זֶ֛ה קׇרְבַּ֥ן אֱלִיאָ֖ב בֶּן־חֵלֹֽן׃ {פ}
verses.text_kjv: And for a sacrifice of peace offerings, two oxen, five rams, five he goats, five lambs of the first year: this was the offering of Eliab the son of Helon.
verses.rendering: As a fellowship sacrifice: two cattle, five rams, five male goats, and five first-year male lambs. This was the offering of Eliab son of Helon.
translator_notes: Eliab's shelamim concludes Zebulun's Day 3 offering and completes the eastern camp division's three-day dedication sequence. All three eastern tribes have now presented.

verses.text_hebrew: בַּיּוֹם֙ הָרְבִיעִ֔י נָשִׂ֖יא לִבְנֵ֣י רְאוּבֵ֑ן אֱלִיצ֖וּר בֶּן־שְׁדֵיאֽוּר׃
verses.text_kjv: On the fourth day Elizur the son of Shedeur, prince of the children of Reuben, did offer:
verses.rendering: On the fourth day, Elizur son of Shedeur, leader of the Reubenites, brought his offering.
translator_notes: Day 4: Elizur (elitsur, 'my God is a rock') son of Shedeur (shedei'ur, 'the Almighty is light'), Reuben's leader. Despite being Jacob's firstborn, Reuben presents fourth — following the camp formation order rather than birth order, since Judah leads the eastern division.

verses.text_hebrew: קׇרְבָּנ֞וֹ קַֽעֲרַת־כֶּ֣סֶף אַחַ֗ת שְׁלֹשִׁ֣ים וּמֵאָה֮ מִשְׁקָלָהּ֒ מִזְרָ֤ק אֶחָד֙ כֶּ֔סֶף שִׁבְעִ֥ים שֶׁ֖קֶל בְּשֶׁ֣קֶל הַקֹּ֑דֶשׁ שְׁנֵיהֶ֣ם ׀ מְלֵאִ֗ים סֹ֛לֶת בְּלוּלָ֥ה בַשֶּׁ֖מֶן לְמִנְחָֽה׃
verses.text_kjv: His offering was one silver charger of the weight of an hundred and thirty shekels, one silver bowl of seventy shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary; both of them full of fine flour mingled with oil for a meat offering:
verses.rendering: His offering consisted of one silver plate weighing one hundred thirty shekels, one silver basin of seventy shekels measured by the sanctuary standard, both full of fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering;
translator_notes: Elizur's silver vessels for Reuben (Day 4): the qa'arat and mizraq with identical weights mark the southern camp's first offering. Reuben the firstborn receives no larger portion.

verses.text_hebrew: כַּ֥ף אַחַ֛ת עֲשָׂרָ֥ה זָהָ֖ב מְלֵאָ֥ה קְטֹֽרֶת׃
verses.text_kjv: One golden spoon of ten shekels, full of incense:
verses.rendering: a gold ladle of ten shekels weight, full of incense;
translator_notes: Reuben's gold kaf of incense — the fourth of twelve identical ladles, maintaining the pattern of equal tribal honor regardless of birth order.

verses.text_hebrew: פַּ֣ר אֶחָ֞ד בֶּן־בָּקָ֗ר אַ֧יִל אֶחָ֛ד כֶּֽבֶשׂ־אֶחָ֥ד בֶּן־שְׁנָת֖וֹ לְעֹלָֽה׃
verses.text_kjv: One young bullock, one ram, one lamb of the first year, for a burnt offering:
verses.rendering: one young bull, one ram, one male lamb in its first year, for a burnt offering;
translator_notes: Reuben's olah: the same three-animal burnt offering — the firstborn tribe receives no larger or more prestigious sacrifice than any other.

verses.text_hebrew: שְׂעִיר־עִזִּ֥ים אֶחָ֖ד לְחַטָּֽאת׃
verses.text_kjv: One kid of the goats for a sin offering:
verses.rendering: one male goat as a purification offering;
translator_notes: Reuben's chattat: one male goat — even Jacob's firstborn tribe requires purification before the newly consecrated sanctuary.

verses.text_hebrew: וּלְזֶ֣בַח הַשְּׁלָמִים֮ בָּקָ֣ר שְׁנַ֒יִם֒ אֵילִ֤ם חֲמִשָּׁה֙ עַתֻּדִ֣ים חֲמִשָּׁ֔ה כְּבָשִׂ֥ים בְּנֵֽי־שָׁנָ֖ה חֲמִשָּׁ֑ה זֶ֛ה קׇרְבַּ֥ן אֱלִיצ֖וּר בֶּן־שְׁדֵיאֽוּר׃ {פ}
verses.text_kjv: And for a sacrifice of peace offerings, two oxen, five rams, five he goats, five lambs of the first year: this was the offering of Elizur the son of Shedeur.
verses.rendering: For a fellowship sacrifice: two cattle, five rams, five male goats, five male lambs in their first year. This was the offering of Elizur son of Shedeur.
translator_notes: Elizur's shelamim concludes Reuben's Day 4 offering, opening the southern camp division's three-day sequence.

verses.text_hebrew: בַּיּוֹם֙ הַחֲמִישִׁ֔י נָשִׂ֖יא לִבְנֵ֣י שִׁמְע֑וֹן שְׁלֻֽמִיאֵ֖ל בֶּן־צוּרִֽישַׁדָּֽי׃
verses.text_kjv: On the fifth day Shelumiel the son of Zurishaddai, prince of the children of Simeon, did offer:
verses.rendering: On the fifth day, Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai, leader of the Simeonites, presented his offering.
translator_notes: Day 5: Shelumiel (shelumi'el, 'at peace with God') son of Zurishaddai (tsurishaddai, 'my Rock is the Almighty'), Simeon's leader. Simeon camps alongside Reuben in the southern division (Num 2:12). The theophoric names — invoking shalom (peace) and shaddai (the Almighty) — reflect the piety embedded in Israelite naming conventions.

verses.text_hebrew: קׇרְבָּנ֞וֹ קַֽעֲרַת־כֶּ֣סֶף אַחַ֗ת שְׁלֹשִׁ֣ים וּמֵאָה֮ מִשְׁקָלָהּ֒ מִזְרָ֤ק אֶחָד֙ כֶּ֔סֶף שִׁבְעִ֥ים שֶׁ֖קֶל בְּשֶׁ֣קֶל הַקֹּ֑דֶשׁ שְׁנֵיהֶ֣ם ׀ מְלֵאִ֗ים סֹ֛לֶת בְּלוּלָ֥ה בַשֶּׁ֖מֶן לְמִנְחָֽה׃
verses.text_kjv: His offering was one silver charger, the weight whereof was an hundred and thirty shekels, one silver bowl of seventy shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary; both of them full of fine flour mingled with oil for a meat offering:
verses.rendering: His offering was a silver plate — one hundred thirty shekels in weight — and a silver basin of seventy shekels by the sanctuary standard, both containing fine flour blended with oil as a grain offering;
translator_notes: Shelumiel's silver vessels for Simeon (Day 5): identical plate and basin weights continue the pattern — the Torah's deliberate repetition honors each tribe individually rather than summarizing with 'each brought the same.'

verses.text_hebrew: כַּ֥ף אַחַ֛ת עֲשָׂרָ֥ה זָהָ֖ב מְלֵאָ֥ה קְטֹֽרֶת׃
verses.text_kjv: One golden spoon of ten shekels, full of incense:
verses.rendering: one gold ladle weighing ten shekels, filled with incense;
translator_notes: Simeon's gold kaf of qetoret — the fifth incense offering in the twelve-day dedication, each one a complete act of worship.

verses.text_hebrew: פַּ֣ר אֶחָ֞ד בֶּן־בָּקָ֗ר אַ֧יִל אֶחָ֛ד כֶּֽבֶשׂ־אֶחָ֥ד בֶּן־שְׁנָת֖וֹ לְעֹלָֽה׃
verses.text_kjv: One young bullock, one ram, one lamb of the first year, for a burnt offering:
verses.rendering: one young bull, one ram, and one yearling male lamb as a burnt offering;
translator_notes: Simeon's olah: bull, ram, yearling lamb — the burnt offering pattern remains unchanged through five successive tribes.

verses.text_hebrew: שְׂעִיר־עִזִּ֥ים אֶחָ֖ד לְחַטָּֽאת׃
verses.text_kjv: One kid of the goats for a sin offering:
verses.rendering: one male goat for a purification offering;
translator_notes: Simeon's chattat: one male goat — the fifth tribe's acknowledgment of the need for ritual cleansing before the LORD.

verses.text_hebrew: וּלְזֶ֣בַח הַשְּׁלָמִים֮ בָּקָ֣ר שְׁנַ֒יִם֒ אֵילִ֤ם חֲמִשָּׁה֙ עַתֻּדִ֣ים חֲמִשָּׁ֔ה כְּבָשִׂ֥ים בְּנֵי־שָׁנָ֖ה חֲמִשָּׁ֑ה זֶ֛ה קׇרְבַּ֥ן שְׁלֻמִיאֵ֖ל בֶּן־צוּרִֽישַׁדָּֽי׃ {פ}
verses.text_kjv: And for a sacrifice of peace offerings, two oxen, five rams, five he goats, five lambs of the first year: this was the offering of Shelumiel the son of Zurishaddai.
verses.rendering: And as a fellowship sacrifice: two cattle, five rams, five male goats, five yearling male lambs. This was the offering of Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai.
translator_notes: Shelumiel's shelamim concludes Simeon's Day 5 offering — the southern camp's second contribution to the altar dedication.

verses.text_hebrew: בַּיּוֹם֙ הַשִּׁשִּׁ֔י נָשִׂ֖יא לִבְנֵ֣י גָ֑ד אֶלְיָסָ֖ף בֶּן־דְּעוּאֵֽל׃
verses.text_kjv: On the sixth day Eliasaph the son of Deuel, prince of the children of Gad, offered:
verses.rendering: On the sixth day, Eliasaph son of Deuel, leader of the Gadites, brought his offering.
translator_notes: Day 6: Eliasaph (elyasaph, 'God has added') son of Deuel (de'u'el, 'knowledge of God'), Gad's leader. Gad completes the southern camp triad with Reuben and Simeon (Num 2:14). The name Deuel appears as 'Reuel' in some manuscripts — a common d/r scribal variation in Hebrew.

verses.text_hebrew: קׇרְבָּנ֞וֹ קַֽעֲרַת־כֶּ֣סֶף אַחַ֗ת שְׁלֹשִׁ֣ים וּמֵאָה֮ מִשְׁקָלָהּ֒ מִזְרָ֤ק אֶחָד֙ כֶּ֔סֶף שִׁבְעִ֥ים שֶׁ֖קֶל בְּשֶׁ֣קֶל הַקֹּ֑דֶשׁ שְׁנֵיהֶ֣ם ׀ מְלֵאִ֗ים סֹ֛לֶת בְּלוּלָ֥ה בַשֶּׁ֖מֶן לְמִנְחָֽה׃
verses.text_kjv: His offering was one silver charger of the weight of an hundred and thirty shekels, a silver bowl of seventy shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary; both of them full of fine flour mingled with oil for a meat offering:
verses.rendering: His offering: one silver plate of one hundred thirty shekels weight, one silver basin at seventy shekels by the sanctuary standard, each filled with fine flour combined with oil for a grain offering;
translator_notes: Eliasaph's silver vessels for Gad (Day 6): the same qa'arat (130 shekels) and mizraq (70 shekels), reinforcing six consecutive days of identical generosity from six different tribes.

verses.text_hebrew: כַּ֥ף אַחַ֛ת עֲשָׂרָ֥ה זָהָ֖ב מְלֵאָ֥ה קְטֹֽרֶת׃
verses.text_kjv: One golden spoon of ten shekels, full of incense:
verses.rendering: a gold ladle of ten shekels, filled with incense;
translator_notes: Gad's gold kaf of incense — the sixth ladle, marking the halfway point of the twelve-day dedication ceremony.

verses.text_hebrew: פַּ֣ר אֶחָ֞ד בֶּן־בָּקָ֗ר אַ֧יִל אֶחָ֛ד כֶּֽבֶשׂ־אֶחָ֥ד בֶּן־שְׁנָת֖וֹ לְעֹלָֽה׃
verses.text_kjv: One young bullock, one ram, one lamb of the first year, for a burnt offering:
verses.rendering: a young bull, one ram, and one first-year male lamb for a burnt offering;
translator_notes: Gad's olah: bull, ram, yearling lamb — the burnt offering at the midpoint of the dedication maintains perfect consistency with all previous days.

verses.text_hebrew: שְׂעִיר־עִזִּ֥ים אֶחָ֖ד לְחַטָּֽאת׃
verses.text_kjv: One kid of the goats for a sin offering:
verses.rendering: a male goat as a purification offering;
translator_notes: Gad's chattat: one male goat — the sixth purification offering, one for each tribe that has presented so far.

verses.text_hebrew: וּלְזֶ֣בַח הַשְּׁלָמִים֮ בָּקָ֣ר שְׁנַ֒יִם֒ אֵילִ֤ם חֲמִשָּׁה֙ עַתֻּדִ֣ים חֲמִשָּׁ֔ה כְּבָשִׂ֥ים בְּנֵי־שָׁנָ֖ה חֲמִשָּׁ֑ה זֶ֛ה קׇרְבַּ֥ן אֶלְיָסָ֖ף בֶּן־דְּעוּאֵֽל׃ {פ}
verses.text_kjv: And for a sacrifice of peace offerings, two oxen, five rams, five he goats, five lambs of the first year: this was the offering of Eliasaph the son of Deuel.
verses.rendering: For a fellowship sacrifice: two cattle, five rams, five male goats, and five first-year male lambs. This was the offering of Eliasaph son of Deuel.
translator_notes: Eliasaph's shelamim concludes Gad's Day 6 offering and completes the southern camp division's three-day dedication sequence. The first half of the twelve tribes has now presented.

verses.text_hebrew: בַּיּוֹם֙ הַשְּׁבִיעִ֔י נָשִׂ֖יא לִבְנֵ֣י אֶפְרָ֑יִם אֱלִֽישָׁמָ֖ע בֶּן־עַמִּיהֽוּד׃
verses.text_kjv: On the seventh day Elishama the son of Ammihud, prince of the children of Ephraim, offered:
verses.rendering: On the seventh day, Elishama son of Ammihud, leader of the Ephraimites, presented his offering.
translator_notes: Day 7: Elishama (elishama, 'my God has heard') son of Ammihud (ammihud, 'my kinsman is glorious'), Ephraim's leader. Ephraim heads the western camp division as the preeminent Joseph tribe (Num 2:18), reflecting Jacob's blessing that placed Ephraim above Manasseh (Gen 48:19-20).

verses.text_hebrew: קׇרְבָּנ֞וֹ קַֽעֲרַת־כֶּ֣סֶף אַחַ֗ת שְׁלֹשִׁ֣ים וּמֵאָה֮ מִשְׁקָלָהּ֒ מִזְרָ֤ק אֶחָד֙ כֶּ֔סֶף שִׁבְעִ֥ים שֶׁ֖קֶל בְּשֶׁ֣קֶל הַקֹּ֑דֶשׁ שְׁנֵיהֶ֣ם ׀ מְלֵאִ֗ים סֹ֛לֶת בְּלוּלָ֥ה בַשֶּׁ֖מֶן לְמִנְחָֽה׃
verses.text_kjv: His offering was one silver charger, the weight whereof was an hundred and thirty shekels, one silver bowl of seventy shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary; both of them full of fine flour mingled with oil for a meat offering:
verses.rendering: His offering was a silver plate weighing one hundred thirty shekels and a silver basin of seventy shekels according to the sanctuary standard, both of them full of fine flour mixed with oil as a grain offering;
translator_notes: Elishama's silver vessels for Ephraim (Day 7): the standardized qa'arat and mizraq weights with the minchah grain mixture, now at the seventh iteration of identical gifts.

verses.text_hebrew: כַּ֥ף אַחַ֛ת עֲשָׂרָ֥ה זָהָ֖ב מְלֵאָ֥ה קְטֹֽרֶת׃
verses.text_kjv: One golden spoon of ten shekels, full of incense:
verses.rendering: one gold ladle, ten shekels, full of incense;
translator_notes: Ephraim's gold kaf of qetoret — the seventh incense ladle, as the western camp begins its three-day dedication sequence.

verses.text_hebrew: פַּ֣ר אֶחָ֞ד בֶּן־בָּקָ֗ר אַ֧יִל אֶחָ֛ד כֶּֽבֶשׂ־אֶחָ֥ד בֶּן־שְׁנָת֖וֹ לְעֹלָֽה׃
verses.text_kjv: One young bullock, one ram, one lamb of the first year, for a burnt offering:
verses.rendering: one young bull, one ram, one male lamb in its first year as a burnt offering;
translator_notes: Ephraim's olah: bull, ram, yearling lamb — the Joseph tribes receive no larger offering despite their prominent double-tribe status.

verses.text_hebrew: שְׂעִיר־עִזִּ֥ים אֶחָ֖ד לְחַטָּֽאת׃
verses.text_kjv: one kid of the goats for a sin offering:
verses.rendering: one male goat for a purification offering;
translator_notes: Ephraim's chattat: one male goat for purification — the seventh tribe's offering mirrors all predecessors exactly.

verses.text_hebrew: וּלְזֶ֣בַח הַשְּׁלָמִים֮ בָּקָ֣ר שְׁנַ֒יִם֒ אֵילִ֤ם חֲמִשָּׁה֙ עַתֻּדִ֣ים חֲמִשָּׁ֔ה כְּבָשִׂ֥ים בְּנֵֽי־שָׁנָ֖ה חֲמִשָּׁ֑ה זֶ֛ה קׇרְבַּ֥ן אֱלִישָׁמָ֖ע בֶּן־עַמִּיהֽוּד׃ {פ}
verses.text_kjv: And for a sacrifice of peace offerings, two oxen, five rams, five he goats, five lambs of the first year: this was the offering of Elishama the son of Ammihud.
verses.rendering: As a fellowship sacrifice: two cattle, five rams, five male goats, five male lambs in their first year. This was the offering of Elishama son of Ammihud.
translator_notes: Elishama's shelamim concludes Ephraim's Day 7 offering, opening the western camp division's three-day dedication sequence.

verses.text_hebrew: בַּיּוֹם֙ הַשְּׁמִינִ֔י נָשִׂ֖יא לִבְנֵ֣י מְנַשֶּׁ֑ה גַּמְלִיאֵ֖ל בֶּן־פְּדָהצֽוּר׃
verses.text_kjv: On the eighth day offered Gamaliel the son of Pedahzur, prince of the children of Manasseh:
verses.rendering: On the eighth day, Gamaliel son of Pedahzur, leader of the Manassites, brought his offering.
translator_notes: Day 8: Gamaliel (gamli'el, 'God is my reward') son of Pedahzur (pedahtsur, 'the Rock has redeemed'), Manasseh's leader. Manasseh follows Ephraim despite being Joseph's actual firstborn — reflecting Jacob's crossed-hand blessing that elevated the younger over the elder (Gen 48:14).

verses.text_hebrew: קׇרְבָּנ֞וֹ קַֽעֲרַת־כֶּ֣סֶף אַחַ֗ת שְׁלֹשִׁ֣ים וּמֵאָה֮ מִשְׁקָלָהּ֒ מִזְרָ֤ק אֶחָד֙ כֶּ֔סֶף שִׁבְעִ֥ים שֶׁ֖קֶל בְּשֶׁ֣קֶל הַקֹּ֑דֶשׁ שְׁנֵיהֶ֣ם ׀ מְלֵאִ֗ים סֹ֛לֶת בְּלוּלָ֥ה בַשֶּׁ֖מֶן לְמִנְחָֽה׃
verses.text_kjv: His offering was one silver charger of the weight of an hundred and thirty shekels, one silver bowl of seventy shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary; both of them full of fine flour mingled with oil for a meat offering:
verses.rendering: His offering: one silver plate at one hundred thirty shekels in weight, one silver basin of seventy shekels by the sanctuary standard — both filled with fine flour stirred into oil for a grain offering;
translator_notes: Gamaliel's silver vessels for Manasseh (Day 8): identical to Ephraim's — neither Joseph tribe receives preferential treatment in the dedication despite their shared patriarch.

verses.text_hebrew: כַּ֥ף אַחַ֛ת עֲשָׂרָ֥ה זָהָ֖ב מְלֵאָ֥ה קְטֹֽרֶת׃
verses.text_kjv: One golden spoon of ten shekels, full of incense:
verses.rendering: a gold ladle at ten shekels weight, filled with incense;
translator_notes: Manasseh's gold kaf of incense — the eighth golden ladle in the sequence, maintaining the standard of equal tribal representation.

verses.text_hebrew: פַּ֣ר אֶחָ֞ד בֶּן־בָּקָ֗ר אַ֧יִל אֶחָ֛ד כֶּֽבֶשׂ־אֶחָ֥ד בֶּן־שְׁנָת֖וֹ לְעֹלָֽה׃
verses.text_kjv: One young bullock, one ram, one lamb of the first year, for a burnt offering:
verses.rendering: one young bull, one ram, and a yearling male lamb for a burnt offering;
translator_notes: Manasseh's olah: the three-animal burnt offering continues unchanged through eight tribes and eight consecutive days of dedication.

verses.text_hebrew: שְׂעִיר־עִזִּ֥ים אֶחָ֖ד לְחַטָּֽאת׃
verses.text_kjv: One kid of the goats for a sin offering:
verses.rendering: one male goat as a purification offering;
translator_notes: Manasseh's chattat: one male goat — the purification offering requirement applies equally to every tribe, the eighth in unbroken sequence.

verses.text_hebrew: וּלְזֶ֣בַח הַשְּׁלָמִים֮ בָּקָ֣ר שְׁנַ֒יִם֒ אֵילִ֤ם חֲמִשָּׁה֙ עַתֻּדִ֣ים חֲמִשָּׁ֔ה כְּבָשִׂ֥ים בְּנֵי־שָׁנָ֖ה חֲמִשָּׁ֑ה זֶ֛ה קׇרְבַּ֥ן גַּמְלִיאֵ֖ל בֶּן־פְּדָהצֽוּר׃ {פ}
verses.text_kjv: And for a sacrifice of peace offerings, two oxen, five rams, five he goats, five lambs of the first year: this was the offering of Gamaliel the son of Pedahzur.
verses.rendering: For a fellowship sacrifice: two cattle, five rams, five male goats, and five yearling male lambs. This was the offering of Gamaliel son of Pedahzur.
translator_notes: Gamaliel's shelamim concludes Manasseh's Day 8 offering — the second of the western camp division's three-day dedication.

verses.text_hebrew: בַּיּוֹם֙ הַתְּשִׁיעִ֔י נָשִׂ֖יא לִבְנֵ֣י בִנְיָמִ֑ן אֲבִידָ֖ן בֶּן־גִּדְעֹנִֽי׃
verses.text_kjv: On the ninth day Abidan the son of Gideoni, prince of the children of Benjamin, offered:
verses.rendering: On the ninth day, Abidan son of Gideoni, leader of the Benjaminites, presented his offering.
translator_notes: Day 9: Abidan (avidan, 'my father has judged' or 'father of judgment') son of Gideoni (gid'oni, 'my hewer/cutter'), Benjamin's leader. Benjamin completes the western camp triad with Ephraim and Manasseh (Num 2:22). Benjamin is Rachel's second son and Jacob's youngest — the 'son of the right hand' (ben-yamin).

verses.text_hebrew: קׇרְבָּנ֞וֹ קַֽעֲרַת־כֶּ֣סֶף אַחַ֗ת שְׁלֹשִׁ֣ים וּמֵאָה֮ מִשְׁקָלָהּ֒ מִזְרָ֤ק אֶחָד֙ כֶּ֔סֶף שִׁבְעִ֥ים שֶׁ֖קֶל בְּשֶׁ֣קֶל הַקֹּ֑דֶשׁ שְׁנֵיהֶ֣ם ׀ מְלֵאִ֗ים סֹ֛לֶת בְּלוּלָ֥ה בַשֶּׁ֖מֶן לְמִנְחָֽה׃
verses.text_kjv: His offering was one silver charger, the weight whereof was an hundred and thirty shekels, one silver bowl of seventy shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary; both of them full of fine flour mingled with oil for a meat offering:
verses.rendering: His offering was one silver plate weighing one hundred thirty shekels, one silver basin of seventy shekels by the sanctuary standard, both containing fine flour blended with oil as a grain offering;
translator_notes: Abidan's silver vessels for Benjamin (Day 9): the ninth identical set of plate and basin, approaching the conclusion of the twelve-day ceremony.

verses.text_hebrew: כַּ֥ף אַחַ֛ת עֲשָׂרָ֥ה זָהָ֖ב מְלֵאָ֥ה קְטֹֽרֶת׃
verses.text_kjv: One golden spoon of ten shekels, full of incense:
verses.rendering: one gold ladle of ten shekels, full of incense;
translator_notes: Benjamin's gold kaf of qetoret — the ninth incense offering, with three tribal offerings still remaining in the dedication sequence.

verses.text_hebrew: פַּ֣ר אֶחָ֞ד בֶּן־בָּקָ֗ר אַ֧יִל אֶחָ֛ד כֶּֽבֶשׂ־אֶחָ֥ד בֶּן־שְׁנָת֖וֹ לְעֹלָֽה׃
verses.text_kjv: One young bullock, one ram, one lamb of the first year, for a burnt offering:
verses.rendering: a young bull, a ram, and a first-year male lamb as a burnt offering;
translator_notes: Benjamin's olah: bull, ram, yearling lamb — the western camp's final burnt offering mirrors all previous ones exactly.

verses.text_hebrew: שְׂעִיר־עִזִּ֥ים אֶחָ֖ד לְחַטָּֽאת׃
verses.text_kjv: One kid of the goats for a sin offering:
verses.rendering: a male goat for a purification offering;
translator_notes: Benjamin's chattat: one male goat for purification — the ninth tribe's identical contribution to the dedication.

verses.text_hebrew: וּלְזֶ֣בַח הַשְּׁלָמִים֮ בָּקָ֣ר שְׁנַ֒יִם֒ אֵילִ֤ם חֲמִשָּׁה֙ עַתֻּדִ֣ים חֲמִשָּׁ֔ה כְּבָשִׂ֥ים בְּנֵי־שָׁנָ֖ה חֲמִשָּׁ֑ה זֶ֛ה קׇרְבַּ֥ן אֲבִידָ֖ן בֶּן־גִּדְעֹנִֽי׃ {פ}
verses.text_kjv: And for a sacrifice of peace offerings, two oxen, five rams, five he goats, five lambs of the first year: this was the offering of Abidan the son of Gideoni.
verses.rendering: As a fellowship sacrifice: two cattle, five rams, five male goats, five first-year male lambs. This was the offering of Abidan son of Gideoni.
translator_notes: Abidan's shelamim concludes Benjamin's Day 9 offering and completes the western camp division's three-day dedication sequence. Nine of twelve tribes have now presented.

verses.text_hebrew: בַּיּוֹם֙ הָעֲשִׂירִ֔י נָשִׂ֖יא לִבְנֵ֣י דָ֑ן אֲחִיעֶ֖זֶר בֶּן־עַמִּישַׁדָּֽי׃
verses.text_kjv: On the tenth day Ahiezer the son of Ammishaddai, prince of the children of Dan, offered:
verses.rendering: On the tenth day, Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai, leader of the Danites, brought his offering.
translator_notes: Day 10: Ahiezer (achi'ezer, 'my brother is help') son of Ammishaddai (ammishaddai, 'my kinsman is the Almighty'), Dan's leader. Dan heads the northern camp — the final camp division — positioned at the rear of the march column (Num 2:25, 10:25).

verses.text_hebrew: קׇרְבָּנ֞וֹ קַֽעֲרַת־כֶּ֣סֶף אַחַ֗ת שְׁלֹשִׁ֣ים וּמֵאָה֮ מִשְׁקָלָהּ֒ מִזְרָ֤ק אֶחָד֙ כֶּ֔סֶף שִׁבְעִ֥ים שֶׁ֖קֶל בְּשֶׁ֣קֶל הַקֹּ֑דֶשׁ שְׁנֵיהֶ֣ם ׀ מְלֵאִ֗ים סֹ֛לֶת בְּלוּלָ֥ה בַשֶּׁ֖מֶן לְמִנְחָֽה׃
verses.text_kjv: His offering was one silver charger, the weight whereof was an hundred and thirty shekels, one silver bowl of seventy shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary; both of them full of fine flour mingled with oil for a meat offering:
verses.rendering: His offering consisted of a silver plate weighing one hundred thirty shekels and a silver basin of seventy shekels measured by the sanctuary standard, both full of fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering;
translator_notes: Ahiezer's silver vessels for Dan (Day 10): the qa'arat and mizraq at standard weights — the tenth of twelve identical offerings approaching the ceremony's conclusion.

verses.text_hebrew: כַּ֥ף אַחַ֛ת עֲשָׂרָ֥ה זָהָ֖ב מְלֵאָ֥ה קְטֹֽרֶת׃
verses.text_kjv: One golden spoon of ten shekels, full of incense:
verses.rendering: a gold ladle weighing ten shekels, filled with incense;
translator_notes: Dan's gold kaf of incense — the tenth golden ladle, as the dedication nears its final three days.

verses.text_hebrew: פַּ֣ר אֶחָ֞ד בֶּן־בָּקָ֗ר אַ֧יִל אֶחָ֛ד כֶּֽבֶשׂ־אֶחָ֥ד בֶּן־שְׁנָת֖וֹ לְעֹלָֽה׃
verses.text_kjv: One young bullock, one ram, one lamb of the first year, for a burnt offering:
verses.rendering: one young bull, one ram, one yearling male lamb for a burnt offering;
translator_notes: Dan's olah: bull, ram, yearling lamb — ten consecutive days of identical burnt offerings underscoring the equality theme that pervades the chapter.

verses.text_hebrew: שְׂעִיר־עִזִּ֥ים אֶחָ֖ד לְחַטָּֽאת׃
verses.text_kjv: One kid of the goats for a sin offering:
verses.rendering: one male goat as a purification offering;
translator_notes: Dan's chattat: one male goat — the tenth purification offering in the twelve-day dedication, maintaining the unchanging pattern.

verses.text_hebrew: וּלְזֶ֣בַח הַשְּׁלָמִים֮ בָּקָ֣ר שְׁנַ֒יִם֒ אֵילִ֤ם חֲמִשָּׁה֙ עַתֻּדִ֣ים חֲמִשָּׁ֔ה כְּבָשִׂ֥ים בְּנֵֽי־שָׁנָ֖ה חֲמִשָּׁ֑ה זֶ֛ה קׇרְבַּ֥ן אֲחִיעֶ֖זֶר בֶּן־עַמִּישַׁדָּֽי׃ {פ}
verses.text_kjv: And for a sacrifice of peace offerings, two oxen, five rams, five he goats, five lambs of the first year: this was the offering of Ahiezer the son of Ammishaddai.
verses.rendering: For a fellowship sacrifice: two cattle, five rams, five male goats, five male lambs in their first year. This was the offering of Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai.
translator_notes: Ahiezer's shelamim concludes Dan's Day 10 offering, beginning the final northern camp division's three-day sequence. Only Asher and Naphtali remain.

verses.text_hebrew: בְּיוֹם֙ עַשְׁתֵּ֣י עָשָׂ֣ר י֔וֹם נָשִׂ֖יא לִבְנֵ֣י אָשֵׁ֑ר פַּגְעִיאֵ֖ל בֶּן־עׇכְרָֽן׃
verses.text_kjv: On the eleventh day Pagiel the son of Ocran, prince of the children of Asher, offered:
verses.rendering: On the eleventh day, Pagiel son of Ocran, leader of the Asherites, presented his offering.
translator_notes: Day 11: Pagiel (pag'i'el, 'encounter with God' or 'God's intervention') son of Ocran (okhran, possibly 'troubled' or 'turbulent'), Asher's leader. Asher is the penultimate tribe in the ceremony, camping in the northern division alongside Dan (Num 2:27).

verses.text_hebrew: קׇרְבָּנ֞וֹ קַֽעֲרַת־כֶּ֣סֶף אַחַ֗ת שְׁלֹשִׁ֣ים וּמֵאָה֮ מִשְׁקָלָהּ֒ מִזְרָ֤ק אֶחָד֙ כֶּ֔סֶף שִׁבְעִ֥ים שֶׁ֖קֶל בְּשֶׁ֣קֶל הַקֹּ֑דֶשׁ שְׁנֵיהֶ֣ם ׀ מְלֵאִ֗ים סֹ֛לֶת בְּלוּלָ֥ה בַשֶּׁ֖מֶן לְמִנְחָֽה׃
verses.text_kjv: His offering was one silver charger, the weight whereof was an hundred and thirty shekels, one silver bowl of seventy shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary; both of them full of fine flour mingled with oil for a meat offering:
verses.rendering: His offering was a silver plate of one hundred thirty shekels weight, a silver basin of seventy shekels by the sanctuary standard, both filled with fine flour combined with oil as a grain offering;
translator_notes: Pagiel's silver vessels for Asher (Day 11): the eleventh identical plate-and-basin set, with only Naphtali's offering remaining to complete the cycle.

verses.text_hebrew: כַּ֥ף אַחַ֛ת עֲשָׂרָ֥ה זָהָ֖ב מְלֵאָ֥ה קְטֹֽרֶת׃
verses.text_kjv: One golden spoon of ten shekels, full of incense:
verses.rendering: one gold ladle, ten shekels in weight, full of incense;
translator_notes: Asher's gold kaf of qetoret — the eleventh incense ladle, nearly completing the twelve-tribe cycle of aromatic offerings.

verses.text_hebrew: פַּ֣ר אֶחָ֞ד בֶּן־בָּקָ֗ר אַ֧יִל אֶחָ֛ד כֶּֽבֶשׂ־אֶחָ֥ד בֶּן־שְׁנָת֖וֹ לְעֹלָֽה׃
verses.text_kjv: One young bullock, one ram, one lamb of the first year, for a burnt offering:
verses.rendering: a young bull, one ram, and one male lamb in its first year as a burnt offering;
translator_notes: Asher's olah: bull, ram, yearling lamb — the penultimate burnt offering, identical to all ten preceding ones.

verses.text_hebrew: שְׂעִיר־עִזִּ֥ים אֶחָ֖ד לְחַטָּֽאת׃
verses.text_kjv: One kid of the goats for a sin offering:
verses.rendering: one male goat for a purification offering;
translator_notes: Asher's chattat: one male goat for purification — the eleventh tribe's offering follows the unvarying pattern of ritual cleansing.

verses.text_hebrew: וּלְזֶ֣בַח הַשְּׁלָמִים֮ בָּקָ֣ר שְׁנַ֒יִם֒ אֵילִ֤ם חֲמִשָּׁה֙ עַתֻּדִ֣ים חֲמִשָּׁ֔ה כְּבָשִׂ֥ים בְּנֵֽי־שָׁנָ֖ה חֲמִשָּׁ֑ה זֶ֛ה קׇרְבַּ֥ן פַּגְעִיאֵ֖ל בֶּן־עׇכְרָֽן׃ {פ}
verses.text_kjv: And for a sacrifice of peace offerings, two oxen, five rams, five he goats, five lambs of the first year: this was the offering of Pagiel the son of Ocran.
verses.rendering: And for a fellowship sacrifice: two cattle, five rams, five male goats, five yearling male lambs. This was the offering of Pagiel son of Ocran.
translator_notes: Pagiel's shelamim concludes Asher's Day 11 offering — only Naphtali's final dedication remains to complete the twelve-day ceremony.

verses.text_hebrew: בְּיוֹם֙ שְׁנֵ֣ים עָשָׂ֣ר י֔וֹם נָשִׂ֖יא לִבְנֵ֣י נַפְתָּלִ֑י אֲחִירַ֖ע בֶּן־עֵינָֽן׃
verses.text_kjv: On the twelfth day Ahira the son of Enan, prince of the children of Naphtali, offered:
verses.rendering: On the twelfth day, Ahira son of Enan, leader of the Naphtalites, brought his offering.
translator_notes: Day 12: Ahira (achira, 'my brother is a friend' or 'brother of evil/misfortune') son of Enan (einan, 'having eyes' or 'springs'), Naphtali's leader. As the twelfth and final tribe, Naphtali completes the altar dedication ceremony. Naphtali camps in the northern division, closing the rear guard of the march (Num 2:29).

verses.text_hebrew: קׇרְבָּנ֞וֹ קַֽעֲרַת־כֶּ֣סֶף אַחַ֗ת שְׁלֹשִׁ֣ים וּמֵאָה֮ מִשְׁקָלָהּ֒ מִזְרָ֤ק אֶחָד֙ כֶּ֔סֶף שִׁבְעִ֥ים שֶׁ֖קֶל בְּשֶׁ֣קֶל הַקֹּ֑דֶשׁ שְׁנֵיהֶ֣ם ׀ מְלֵאִ֗ים סֹ֛לֶת בְּלוּלָ֥ה בַשֶּׁ֖מֶן לְמִנְחָֽה׃
verses.text_kjv: His offering was one silver charger, the weight whereof was an hundred and thirty shekels, one silver bowl of seventy shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary; both of them full of fine flour mingled with oil for a meat offering:
verses.rendering: His offering: one silver plate weighing one hundred thirty shekels, one silver basin of seventy shekels according to the sanctuary standard, each of them full of fine flour blended with oil for a grain offering;
translator_notes: Ahira's silver vessels for Naphtali (Day 12): the final identical plate-and-basin set, completing the twelve-tribe cycle of generosity. All twelve tribes have now contributed equally.

verses.text_hebrew: כַּ֥ף אַחַ֛ת עֲשָׂרָ֥ה זָהָ֖ב מְלֵאָ֥ה קְטֹֽרֶת׃
verses.text_kjv: One golden spoon of ten shekels, full of incense:
verses.rendering: a gold ladle of ten shekels weight, filled with incense;
translator_notes: Naphtali's gold kaf of qetoret — the twelfth and final incense ladle, completing the full set of twelve golden vessels filled with fragrant worship.

verses.text_hebrew: פַּ֣ר אֶחָ֞ד בֶּן־בָּקָ֗ר אַ֧יִל אֶחָ֛ד כֶּֽבֶשׂ־אֶחָ֥ד בֶּן־שְׁנָת֖וֹ לְעֹלָֽה׃
verses.text_kjv: One young bullock, one ram, one lamb of the first year, for a burnt offering:
verses.rendering: one young bull, one ram, and one male lamb in its first year for a burnt offering;
translator_notes: Naphtali's olah: bull, ram, yearling lamb — the final burnt offering in the twelve-day dedication matches all eleven predecessors in precise conformity.

verses.text_hebrew: שְׂעִיר־עִזִּ֥ים אֶחָ֖ד לְחַטָּֽאת׃
verses.text_kjv: One kid of the goats for a sin offering:
verses.rendering: a male goat as a purification offering;
translator_notes: Naphtali's chattat: one male goat — the twelfth and final purification offering, completing the full cycle of tribal dedication.

verses.text_hebrew: וּלְזֶ֣בַח הַשְּׁלָמִים֮ בָּקָ֣ר שְׁנַ֒יִם֒ אֵילִ֤ם חֲמִשָּׁה֙ עַתֻּדִ֣ים חֲמִשָּׁ֔ה כְּבָשִׂ֥ים בְּנֵֽי־שָׁנָ֖ה חֲמִשָּׁ֑ה זֶ֛ה קׇרְבַּ֥ן אֲחִירַ֖ע בֶּן־עֵינָֽן׃ {פ}
verses.text_kjv: And for a sacrifice of peace offerings, two oxen, five rams, five he goats, five lambs of the first year: this was the offering of Ahira the son of Enan.
verses.rendering: As a fellowship sacrifice: two cattle, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs in their first year. This was the offering of Ahira son of Enan.
translator_notes: Ahira's shelamim concludes Naphtali's Day 12 offering, bringing the entire twelve-day altar dedication to its close. All four camp divisions — east, south, west, north — have completed their presentations in order.

verses.text_hebrew: זֹ֣את ׀ חֲנֻכַּ֣ת הַמִּזְבֵּ֗חַ בְּיוֹם֙ הִמָּשַׁ֣ח אֹת֔וֹ מֵאֵ֖ת נְשִׂיאֵ֣י יִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל קַעֲרֹ֨ת כֶּ֜סֶף שְׁתֵּ֣ים עֶשְׂרֵ֗ה מִֽזְרְקֵי־כֶ֙סֶף֙ שְׁנֵ֣ים עָשָׂ֔ר כַּפּ֥וֹת זָהָ֖ב שְׁתֵּ֥ים עֶשְׂרֵֽה׃
verses.text_kjv: This was the dedication of the altar, in the day when it was anointed, by the princes of Israel: twelve chargers of silver, twelve silver bowls, twelve spoons of gold:
verses.rendering: This was the total for the altar's dedication on the day it was anointed, contributed by the leaders of Israel: twelve silver plates, twelve silver basins, twelve gold ladles —
translator_notes: The summary section opens with zo't chanukkat hammizbe'ach (this was the dedication of the altar), tallying the collective gifts. The twelve-times-three vessel count — qa'arot kesef (silver plates), mizreqei kesef (silver basins), kappot zahav (gold ladles) — demonstrates the unified generosity of all twelve tribes.

verses.text_hebrew: שְׁלֹשִׁ֣ים וּמֵאָ֗ה הַקְּעָרָ֤ה הָֽאַחַת֙ כֶּ֔סֶף וְשִׁבְעִ֖ים הַמִּזְרָ֣ק הָאֶחָ֑ד כֹּ֚ל כֶּ֣סֶף הַכֵּלִ֔ים אַלְפַּ֥יִם וְאַרְבַּע־מֵא֖וֹת בְּשֶׁ֥קֶל הַקֹּֽדֶשׁ׃
verses.text_kjv: Each charger of silver weighing an hundred and thirty shekels, each bowl seventy: all the silver vessels weighed two thousand and four hundred shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary:
verses.rendering: each silver plate weighing one hundred thirty shekels, each basin seventy — the total silver of all the vessels came to two thousand four hundred shekels by the sanctuary standard;
translator_notes: The total silver: 12 plates at 130 shekels + 12 basins at 70 shekels = 2,400 sheqalim (approximately 27.5 kg or 60 lbs by the sanctuary standard). The precise accounting — kol kesef hakkelim (all the silver of the vessels) — reflects the meticulous record-keeping expected for sacred donations.

verses.text_hebrew: כַּפּ֨וֹת זָהָ֤ב שְׁתֵּים־עֶשְׂרֵה֙ מְלֵאֹ֣ת קְטֹ֔רֶת עֲשָׂרָ֧ה עֲשָׂרָ֛ה הַכַּ֖ף בְּשֶׁ֣קֶל הַקֹּ֑דֶשׁ כׇּל־זְהַ֥ב הַכַּפּ֖וֹת עֶשְׂרִ֥ים וּמֵאָֽה׃
verses.text_kjv: The golden spoons were twelve, full of incense, weighing ten shekels apiece, after the shekel of the sanctuary: all the gold of the spoons was an hundred and twenty shekels.
verses.rendering: the twelve gold ladles full of incense, each ladle weighing ten shekels by the sanctuary standard — the total gold of the ladles came to one hundred twenty shekels.
translator_notes: The total gold: 12 ladles at 10 shekels each = 120 sheqalim (approximately 1.4 kg or 3 lbs). The phrase asarah asarah hakkaf (ten-ten per ladle — ten shekels each) uses the distributive repetition typical of Hebrew census and inventory language. Though less by weight than the silver, gold's far greater value made this a substantial combined contribution.

verses.text_hebrew: כׇּל־הַבָּקָ֨ר לָעֹלָ֜ה שְׁנֵ֧ים עָשָׂ֣ר פָּרִ֗ים אֵילִ֤ם שְׁנֵים־עָשָׂר֙ כְּבָשִׂ֧ים בְּנֵֽי־שָׁנָ֛ה שְׁנֵ֥ים עָשָׂ֖ר וּמִנְחָתָ֑ם וּשְׂעִירֵ֥י עִזִּ֛ים שְׁנֵ֥ים עָשָׂ֖ר לְחַטָּֽאת׃
verses.text_kjv: All the oxen for the burnt offering were twelve bullocks, the rams twelve, the lambs of the first year twelve, with their meat offering: and the kids of the goats for sin offering twelve.
verses.rendering: The total livestock for the burnt offerings: twelve bulls, twelve rams, twelve yearling male lambs with their grain offerings, and twelve male goats for the purification offering.
translator_notes: The olah (burnt offering) totals reflect perfect symmetry: twelve parim (bulls), twelve eilim (rams), twelve kevasim (lambs) — one from each tribe — plus their accompanying minchah (grain offering). The chattat adds twelve se'irei izzim (male goats) for purification. The number twelve pervades every category, reinforcing the all-Israel character of the dedication.

verses.text_hebrew: וְכֹ֞ל בְּקַ֣ר ׀ זֶ֣בַח הַשְּׁלָמִ֗ים עֶשְׂרִ֣ים וְאַרְבָּעָה֮ פָּרִים֒ אֵילִ֤ם שִׁשִּׁים֙ עַתֻּדִ֣ים שִׁשִּׁ֔ים כְּבָשִׂ֥ים בְּנֵי־שָׁנָ֖ה שִׁשִּׁ֑ים זֹ֚את חֲנֻכַּ֣ת הַמִּזְבֵּ֔חַ אַחֲרֵ֖י הִמָּשַׁ֥ח אֹתֽוֹ׃
verses.text_kjv: And all the oxen for the sacrifice of the peace offerings were twenty and four bullocks, the rams sixty, the he goats sixty, the lambs of the first year sixty. This was the dedication of the altar, after that it was anointed.
verses.rendering: The total livestock for the fellowship sacrifices: twenty-four bulls, sixty rams, sixty male goats, sixty yearling male lambs. This was the dedication of the altar after it had been anointed.
translator_notes: The zevach hashelamim (fellowship sacrifice) totals are the largest category: 24 parim (bulls — 2 per tribe), 60 eilim (rams — 5 per tribe), 60 attudim (male goats — 5 per tribe), 60 kevasim (yearling lambs — 5 per tribe) = 204 animals total. The closing phrase zo't chanukkat hammizbe'ach acharei himmashach oto (this was the altar's dedication after it was anointed) forms an inclusio with v. 84.

verses.text_hebrew: וּבְבֹ֨א מֹשֶׁ֜ה אֶל־אֹ֣הֶל מוֹעֵד֮ לְדַבֵּ֣ר אִתּוֹ֒ וַיִּשְׁמַ֨ע אֶת־הַקּ֜וֹל מִדַּבֵּ֣ר אֵלָ֗יו מֵעַ֤ל הַכַּפֹּ֙רֶת֙ אֲשֶׁר֙ עַל־אֲרֹ֣ן הָעֵדֻ֔ת מִבֵּ֖ין שְׁנֵ֣י הַכְּרֻבִ֑ים וַיְדַבֵּ֖ר אֵלָֽיו׃ {פ}
verses.text_kjv: And when Moses was gone into the tabernacle of the congregation to speak with him, then he heard the voice of one speaking unto him from off the mercy seat that was upon the ark of testimony, from between the two cherubims: and he spake unto him.
verses.rendering: When Moses entered the Tent of Meeting to speak with Him, he heard the Voice speaking to him from above the atonement cover on the Ark of the Testimony, from between the two cherubim — and it spoke to him.
translator_notes: The chapter's dramatic climax: Moses enters the ohel mo'ed (Tent of Meeting) and hears haqqol middabber elav (the Voice speaking to him) from above the kapporet (atonement cover/mercy seat) on the aron ha'edut (Ark of the Testimony), from between the two keruvim (cherubim). The dedication is validated by divine speech — the Tabernacle's purpose is fulfilled when God inhabits the consecrated space and communicates from it. The participle middabber (speaking, ongoing speech) suggests continuous divine communication, not a single utterance.

key_terms.semantic_range: mercy seat, propitiatory cover, place of atonement, covering lid
key_terms.note: From k-p-r (to cover, atone, make propitiation). The kapporet is both the physical gold lid on the Ark and the theological locus where atonement occurs — where God's presence meets human need. The Voice emanates from this precise spot.

key_terms.semantic_range: cherubim, angelic guardians, winged throne-bearers, celestial attendants
key_terms.note: The keruvim are not the chubby infants of Western art but formidable composite beings — winged guardians who flank God's throne. Their wings stretch over the kapporet, creating the space from which the divine Voice speaks.
